mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior quarter lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, which supports higher S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts coincide with stable or lower CPIAUCSL inflation, and it fails when cuts occur during inflation shocks.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are negative in most periods when FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, or if the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration that weakens household income expectations and risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS is also decelerating, and it fails if unemployment rises without broader payroll loss.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is flat or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger consumer spending and housing demand, which raises near-term earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when sales improve but housing activity does not confirm broad demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS and HOUST improvement does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if 60d SPX returns are not positive in a majority of joint-positive months.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in INDPRO after a prior weakness signals accelerating industrial activity and cyclical earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice near-term growth. The signal fails if industrial production gains are already fully priced or occur alongside broad demand deterioration.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 5d SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases, or if Rank IC stays below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL indicates an inflation-adjusted easing backdrop that reduces real financing pressure and supports equities over the next 60 trading days. The signal is negative if inflation rises fast enough to force an imminent policy reaction.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is below 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-rate, low-inflation regime does not yield above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if performance reverses when CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above 3.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in HOUST signals residential construction momentum and supports cyclical growth expectations, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails when housing starts rise only because of one-off volatility and do not persist for at least two consecutive months.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month HOUST spikes are followed by negative 20d SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal is not persistent across consecutive housing upswings.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE rises, labor-market deterioration reduces expected consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest after the labor data have been weakening for at least two months and fails if payrolls improve despite unemployment noise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises in the same window
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS decline and UNRATE rise episodes do not produce below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the combined signal is weaker than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when RSAFS contracts while GDPC1 growth is slowing, the economy is losing nominal demand momentum, which should pressure earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if GDP slows but retail sales remain resilient.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth falls below its prior 4-quarter average and RSAFS declines month over month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weak GDPC1 growth and falling RSAFS do not coincide with negative 20d SPX returns more than half the time, or if the signal disappears when retail sales are flat.
